intro: Tertullian (c. 155–220) was the first great theologian to write in Latin, coining the terms trinitas, substantia, and persona that shaped Trinitarian theology. Born in Carthage, he championed orthodoxy against Marcion, Praxeas, and the philosophers. His Apology defended Christianity before Roman courts, while Against Marcion (Books IV–V) provides the earliest extended verse-by-verse engagement with Luke’s Gospel and the Pauline letters, refuting Marcion’s dualist canon.
